meta.title: Couples Therapy This Week | PhD & PsyD | Brooklyn Austin Miami
meta.description: Couples therapy with a doctoral therapist this week. PhD/PsyD only, real coordinator match, $280-380/session, OON reimbursement filed for you. Brooklyn, Austin, Miami, online.

hero.h1: Couples therapy this week, with a PhD or PsyD therapist.
hero.subhead: Couples come to us for all kinds of reasons. The argument that keeps coming back. The work of rebuilding trust after a betrayal. The slow drift into feeling more like roommates than partners. Wherever you are, your first joint session can be this week. Every couples therapist on our team holds a PhD or PsyD.
hero.trustLine: ✓ First session this week ✓ $280 to $380 per session ✓ Out-of-network claim filed for you
hero.imageAlt: Two seated chairs in a WTG couples therapy office

treat.h2: Couples therapy, with the rest of our team behind it
treat.subhead: Six ways our doctoral team supports couples and the lives they're navigating together. Every clinician PhD or PsyD.

cards.title: Couples therapy
cards.body: Same-week first joint session, in person or online. For couples who want to fight less, talk more, and feel close again.

cards.title: Premarital and new chapters
cards.body: Marriage, parenthood, fertility, careers, blended families. Get on the same page before life gets harder.

cards.title: Trust repair after betrayal
cards.body: Structured doctoral support after infidelity or broken trust. Both partners, together, with a clear path.

cards.title: LGBTQ+ affirmative couples
cards.body: Same-sex, polyamorous, open relationships, and queer marriages. Affirmative from intake forward.

cards.title: Individual therapy alongside
cards.body: When one partner also needs solo work. Same practice, same doctoral standard, often the same care plan.

cards.title: Psychiatry on the same team
cards.body: When one partner's anxiety, ADHD, or depression is showing up in the relationship, our psychiatrists step in. One care plan, not two.

comparison.h2: How couples therapy at WTG compares to the rest of the field
comparison.subhead: What you'll get from our competitors versus what happens at WTG.

rows.them: An online therapy app pairs you with the next available therapist.
rows.us: A real coordinator picks a doctoral couples therapist for both of you, on a five-minute intake call.

rows.them: Master's-level therapist with a weekend Gottman or EFT certification.
rows.us: PhD or PsyD trained in evidence-based couples work as part of doctoral curriculum, not through a short course.

rows.them: You scroll a directory and pick a name based on a headshot.
rows.us: We confirm your match in 24 hours, with both partners' schedules and working styles factored in from session one.

rows.them: Generic 'communication tips' regardless of what you're walking in with.
rows.us: Therapy shaped around your specific dynamic, whether that's repair after betrayal, conflict de-escalation, intimacy rebuilding, or parenthood transitions.

rows.them: You handle the out-of-network paperwork alone and hope for reimbursement.
rows.us: Reimbursify files your OON claim for you, and most plans pay back 50 to 80 percent of the session fee.

howItWorks.h2: Four steps from your first call to your first joint session
howItWorks.subhead: It takes less than five minutes to start. No long forms, no chatbots, a real person answers and asks a few questions about what's going on.

steps.title: Call or tap to book
steps.body: Reach our patient coordinator by phone or online. A real person gets back to you the same day, on a five-minute call.

steps.title: Get matched in 24 hours
steps.body: We pair you with a doctoral couples therapist for your goals, both partners' schedules, and how each of you likes to work. Match confirmed within 24 hours.

steps.title: First joint session
steps.body: Both of you meet your therapist together, in person or over secure video, often the same week.

steps.title: Make progress
steps.body: Concrete repair language and structures you'll use between sessions, with check-ins so you can feel the change at home.

whyWTG.subhead: Where doctoral training meets actual couples work.

bullets.title: Doctoral, couples-experienced clinicians
bullets.body: Only PhD and PsyD therapists with couples-specific training. Not master's-level with a weekend Gottman or EFT certification.

bullets.title: Your therapist works for the relationship, not either partner
bullets.body: No taking sides, no ganging up, no being told one of you is the problem. Each of you should feel heard, and each of you should be challenged in the places that move the relationship forward.

bullets.title: Both of you in the room from session one
bullets.body: No 'come to a screening session alone, bring your partner later.' Joint work starts at session 1, the way couples work is meant to be.

bullets.title: Real coordinator match in 24 hours
bullets.body: Not a quiz, not an algorithm. A person on our team listens to where your relationship is and pairs you with the doctoral therapist who's the right fit for both of you. Match confirmed within 24 hours.

bullets.title: Same therapist for as long as you're both in the work
bullets.body: We match you once. You don't restart with a new clinician every time someone leaves the practice.

clinicians.eyebrow: Meet a few of our therapists
clinicians.h2: Doctoral couples specialists across Brooklyn, Austin, Miami, and online
clinicians.subhead: Our coordinator can pair you with any doctoral therapist on the team who works with couples. A few of them are featured below.

midCTA.h2: Both of you, in the room or on Zoom, this week.
midCTA.body: Same-week first joint sessions across Brooklyn, Austin, Miami, and online. The hardest part is the two of you deciding to do this together.

pricing.subhead: We're a cash-pay practice. No insurer dictating how many sessions you get, no diagnosis code filed to your permanent medical record.
pricing.reimbursement: Many patients have out-of-network benefits. We partner with Reimbursify, an independent service that submits your out-of-network claim to your insurer for you. Most plans pay back 50 to 80 percent of the session fee.
steps: We charge you the session fee at the time of service.
We send you a superbill you can submit to your insurance.
Reimbursify files the claim for you. You get reimbursed directly.

reviews.quote: Dr. Selling and the team at WTG are the best! Beautiful office space in Brooklyn.
reviews.author: Michael F.

reviews.quote: I highly recommend Dr. Trubowitz, she's helped our family resolve some longstanding issues. This is the best group of doctors in Austin that I've worked with. The space is also beautiful and relaxing.
reviews.author: Rebecca B.

reviews.quote: This office is unlike any other therapy office I've ever seen. Totally clean, beautiful curved wood everywhere, complimentary tea and water. Particularly impressed with the group therapy room. Just a really nice place to be.
reviews.author: Jake F.

faq.h2: Everything couples ask before the first session

items.q: How much does couples therapy cost, and how long will it take?
items.a: Sessions are $280 to $380 each. Most couples meet weekly to start, often shifting to every other week once the early momentum builds. Couples doing focused repair (after a betrayal, around communication, around de-escalation) often see meaningful change in 12 to 20 sessions. Couples working on deeper attachment patterns may stay longer. Your therapist will set goals with you in the first sessions and check progress regularly. We're not in the business of keeping couples in therapy forever.

items.q: Do you take my insurance for couples therapy?
items.a: We're a cash-pay practice, so we don't bill insurance directly. Most couples use out-of-network benefits and get reimbursed 50 to 80 percent of the session fee. We give you a superbill and partner with Reimbursify, a claim-filing service, that submits it to your insurer for you.

items.q: What if my partner doesn't want to come yet?
items.a: Start solo. Many couples begin with one partner doing individual work first. We can pivot to joint sessions when your partner is ready, or your coordinator can suggest how to invite them into the work.

items.q: Will the therapist take sides between us?
items.a: No. Our couples therapists work for the relationship, not for either partner. Both of you should feel heard, both of you should feel challenged when it's useful, and neither of you should feel ganged up on. Tell your therapist directly if you ever feel that's slipping; that conversation is part of the work.

items.q: What happens in the first joint session?
items.a: Both of you will meet your therapist together. Expect questions about how you met, how things have been lately, and what each of you hopes to get out of therapy. No homework on day one. Just an honest start to the work, with both of you in the room.

form.eyebrow: Ready to take the next step together?
form.h2: Tell us a little about what you're looking for
form.subhead: Our patient coordinator will call or email you back the same day. No waitlists, no chatbots, no pressure. The first joint session can be this week.
form.consent: By leaving your phone or email, you are consenting to receiving communications from us via text or email and that may include communications about your appointments, services, and marketing information related to our services. You may opt out of marketing information at any time. Your contact information will never be sold to any third party and will only be shared to the extent necessary to perform any services on your behalf.
form.phoneFallback: or call 786-692-6547 to speak with our coordinator now.
form.reassurance: Same-day response. No waitlists, no runaround.
